title: Trafigura Signs Offtake Agreement for Advanced Sustainable Aviation Fuel Produced from Biogas
content: Trafigura has signed a binding six-year offtake agreement with a subsidiary of Syzygy Plasmonics to purchase the entire output of advanced Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) from the planned "NovaSAF-1" facility in Uruguay. Targeted to begin deliveries in 2028, this project will be the worldâ€™s first electrified biogas-to-SAF plant, utilizing renewable electricity and biogas from the Estancias Del Lago complex. The fuel is expected to meet Renewable Fuels of Non-Biological Origin (RFNBO) standards, offering a 90% reduction in lifecycle emissions compared to fossil jet fuel. This partnership provides Syzygy with the commercial certainty required to secure financing for construction. For Trafigura, the deal secures a supply of compliant low-carbon fuel to help aviation clients meet strict regulatory mandates in Europe and the UK, while reinforcing its strategy to support emerging technologies.
author: Analysts Account
